description_long: This is a large, elongated barn structure with a steeply pitched gable roof, featuring wooden shingles and prominent ridge decorations at both ends. The roof extends well beyond the walls, creating a deep overhang supported by a series of evenly spaced vertical posts along the perimeter. The exterior siding is composed of horizontal wooden planks, giving the barn a rustic and traditional appearance. There are no visible windows or doors on the long sides, and the gable ends are solid with decorative wooden crosspieces at the apex. The barn sits on a grassy base, suggesting it is elevated slightly above ground level. The overall design is reminiscent of Scandinavian or Viking longhouse architecture, emphasizing durability and simplicity.
description: A large, traditional barn with a steep gable roof, deep overhangs, and horizontal wooden siding supported by vertical posts. The structure is solid and rustic, with decorative ridge elements and no visible windows or doors.
description_view_0: Front gable end view showing the steep roof pitch, decorative ridge crosspiece, and deep overhang supported by vertical posts.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the long expanse of the barn, horizontal wooden siding, and a continuous row of supporting posts beneath the overhanging roof.
description_view_2: Rear gable end view, nearly identical to the front, with a steep roof, decorative ridge element, and deep overhang.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring the other side, with horizontal siding and a row of vertical posts supporting the roof overhang.
attribute_roof_type: steep gable roof with extended eaves and decorative ridge elements
attribute_door_style: single large hinged door implied at the gable end, not visibly detailed
attribute_window_arrangement: no visible windows on any side
